description_long: This is a single-story bungalow with a beige exterior and a prominent, steeply pitched gable roof covered in grayish-brown shingles. The front facade features a small covered porch with steps leading up to a red front door, flanked by white columns and a tiled entryway. Black shutters accentuate the symmetrically arranged windows on both the front and rear sides, while the triangular gable above the porch includes a central window. The side elevations are plain, with no windows or additional architectural details visible. The roof structure is complex, with intersecting gables creating a visually interesting silhouette. The overall appearance is neat and traditional, suitable for a suburban or residential neighborhood.
description: A beige, single-story bungalow with a steep gable roof, black shutters, and a small covered front porch. Symmetrical windows and a tiled entryway give it a classic, suburban look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, red door, tiled steps, and symmetrical windows with black shutters.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain beige wall and the depth of the gable roof, with the porch partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of windows and the continuation of the gable roof, with no porch or entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain wall and the roof's profile with the porch just visible at the edge.
